concerns.text: The plan cites 'snap-cornell/twitter-roberta-base-sentiment-dataset' as the source for historical sentiment scores. This dataset (SNAP-Cornell Twitter Sentiment Corpus) is a static, labeled corpus of ~1.6M tweets from 2009 used for model training/evaluation, NOT a time-series archive with metadata (timestamps) required to aggregate sentiment by quarter or align with GDP/UNRATE data. Using this dataset makes the temporal analysis (lead/lag) impossible as the data lacks the necessary temporal dimension for the specified research question.

The plan proposes using 'huggingartists/freddie-dredd' (a dataset of music artist data) as a wrapper to access FRED macroeconomic indicators (GDP, UNRATE). This is an inappropriate dataset citation; the dataset contains no economic time-series data. While the plan mentions a fallback to the FRED API, relying on a music dataset as the primary 'verified' source for economic indicators is a fundamental data resource mismatch that prevents the ingestion step from functioning as described.
